name: Cafe Vienna Mix
directions: Stir instant coffee, sugar, dry milk powder and cinnamon together.
Process in a blender until powdered.
Use 2 teaspoons to one cup of hot water.

name: Dutch Advocaat
directions: Beat the egg yolks, fine sugar and vanilla extract until the mixture turns  a very smooth, pale yellow colour.
The mixture will be quite thick in its  consistency.
Add the Brandewijn very slowly, keeping your mixer at the  lowest possible speed.
Completely incorporate the brandy into the egg  mixture. Place the Advocaat into the top portion of your double boiler.
Keep your heat on low and slowly, warm the Advocaat. Do not allow the  mixture to pass the warm point.

name: Orange Crystal Russian Tea
directions: Mix ingredients well.
Store in covered jar.
For Tea: Use 1 rounded Tbsp of mixture to  each 8 oz. of hot water.

name: Irish Cream II
directions: Dissolve sugar in water and bring to boil.
Do not overboil.
Cool.
Mix all  ingredients together and put in refrigerator for 2 hours.
It's then OK to  drink.
Keep in refrigerator due to cream content.

name: Bourbon Fog
directions: Mix thoroughly in a punch bowl and serve with a little ice in Old Fashioned or Collins glasses.

name: Tom Collins
directions: Pour into the tallest glass you have, add ice, top with soda and stir.

name: The Claridge
directions: shake with ice and strain into a cocktail glass.

name: Everybody's Irish
directions: Stir with ice, strain into a cocktail glass and suspend the olive.

name: Italian Soda
directions: Fill a glass with ice, add syrup, soda water and half-and-half or milk.
Stir to combine flavors.
Top with whipped cream if desired.

name: Limoncello , Lemoncella
directions: Wash the lemons well with a vegetable brush and hot water to remove any residue of pesticides or wax.
Pat the lemons dry.
Using a vegetable peeler, peel the lemons.
If you do get some of the pith with the zest, carefully scrape it away with the tip of a knife.
Fill the jar with one bottle of the vodka and the zest.
Cover the jar and let it sit forty days at room temperature in a dark cabinet.
Combine the sugar and water in a saucepan, bring it to a boil and cook until thickened, about five minutes.
Let the syrup cool before adding it to the limoncello mixture, along with the other bottle of vodka.
Cover and return to the cupboard for another 40 days.
Strain the limoncello into bottles and discard the lemon zest.
Store them in a cupboard or the freezer.

name: My Mom's Plum Brandy
directions: Rinse the plums (do not scrub away the white bloom) and cut out the pits.
Place in a large crock or one or more large jars.
Pour in as much of the sugar as the container can hold.
Cover loosely (I believe the traditional cover is several layers of cheesecloth but I've done fine with a regular lid that is left unscrewed) and let it ferment at room temperature for six weeks.
As the mixture liquefies keep adding sugar until it is used up.
Strain the liquid (reserving the pulp) and mix it with the whiskey and bottle it up.
My mother always uses a cheap whiskey and allowed the mixture to age in the bottle for many months.
However, using a good whiskey makes it drinkable right away--especially important for the first batch.
It keeps indefinitely--if you make enough.

similar.name: Black Currant Liqueur, Liqueur De Cassis

name: Mead
directions: Wash the lemons and peel them thinly.
Remove the pith.
Slice the lemons and place them with the peel and sugar in a sufficiently large vessel.
Bring half of the water to the boil and pour it over the lemons, peel and sugar.
Stir and leave to stand covered for a while.
Add the rest of the water cold.
When the liquid is lukewarm add the yeast.
Keep the mead at room temperature until it starts to ferment,about one day.
Put a couple of raisins and 1 tsp of sugar into clean bottles, and strain the mead into the bottles.
Loosely cork the bottles and store them in a cool place.
The mead is ready when the raisins rise to the surface.

name: Greek Lightning
directions: Pour into mixing cup with 2 or 3 ice cubes, and chill to make shooter.
Strain into shot glass or test tube.

name: Bloody Shame
directions: Mix V8, lemon juice, pepper and Worcester sauce.
Pour over crushed ice in a tall glass.
Garnish with a sprinkle of ground pepper, a slice of tomato and stalk of celery.
Serve at once.

name: Cafe Vienna
directions: Mix together.
Store in a small canister.
Use 1 tablespoon per mug of hot water.

name: Low-Fat, Low-Cal Cafe Vienna
directions: Mix the coffee, creamer, and cinnamon.
Store in a small plastic canister.
To serve, put 1 1/2 teaspoons of mix in a mug of hot water.
Add 1 packet sweetner.
Stir and enjoy.

name: cafe' viennese
directions: stir all ingredients together until well blended.
To serve, use 1 Tbsp per cup of hot water, or to taste.

name: Boerenjongens
directions: In large saucepan, combine raisins, water, and cinnamon stick and bring to a boil.
Turn down heat and simmer for about 20 minutes.
Add sugar and stir until dissolved.
Remove from heat and cool.
Stir in brandy, and remove cinnamon stick.
Pour into 4 clean pint jars and seal tightly.
Let stand at least 3 months for the best results.

name: Swiss Mocha Instant Coffee Mix
directions: Mix well.
(This can be done in a food processor for finer texture but it is not necessary).
Use 3 teaspoons per mug of boiling water (more or less).

name: Hot Buttered Rum
directions: With mixer or food processor, cream butter.
Gradually add dark sugar then powdered sugar, then ice cream.
Add nutmeg and cinnamon last.
Spoon 1 large heaping tablespoon of mixture into a mug.
Add 1 jigger of dark rum and hot water to fill the cup.
Add cinnamon stick.
The rest of the mixture may be put into a sealed container and kept in refrigerator for future use.

similar.name: Hot Buttered Rum from Saveur Magazine

name: Huckle-My-Butt
directions: Stir thoroughly.

name: The Vanilla Sky
directions: In a cocktail shaker, muddle cranberries with the back of a spoon.
Fill the shaker with ice and add the vodka and amaretto.
Shake sharply and stran into a Martini glass.

name: Hot Irish Mocha
directions: Add first three ingredients in a saucepan; stir until well blended and smooth.
Add coffee and heat mixture on low, stirring constantly, until the cocoa and sugar are dissolved (do not bring to a boil).
Stir in Grand Mariner and Bailey's Irish Cream.
Heat over moderate heat, stirring constantly, until hot but not boiling.
Pour into 6 coffee mugs.

name: Thick Spanish Chocolate
directions: Mix together and cook over a double boiler until mixture comes to a boil, mixing well with a wire whisk to get a creamy effect.
Pour in small cups.
Serve hot.

similar.name: Chocolate Caliente - Spanish Hot Chocolate Too

name: Irish Car Bomb
directions: Find a glass that a shot glass will fit into then fill it up with guiness.
Then fill a shotglass with 1/2 Bailey's and 1/2 Jameson Irish Whiskey.
Drop the full shotglass into the glass of guiness and drink the concoction as fast as you can.
(You're supposed to guzzle it down because the mixture begins to curdle pretty quickly and it looks sorta gross).
Believe it or not- delicious!

name: Hot Irish Kiss
directions: Rim glass with pink sugar, pour hot coffee into glass, add remaining ingredients, except whipped cream, top with whipped cream and sprinkle lightly with cinnamon.

name: Gluhwein
directions: Simmer on stove.
Pour in glass.
Drink and Enjoy!

name: Irish Cream (Grand Pa VanPelt's recipe)
directions: Add all ingredients into blender.
Mix until well blended.
Refrigerate and enjoy.

name: Coquito
directions: Blend all ingredients in a blender on high for 5 minutes.
Refrigerate.
Serve cold, sip and enjoy.

name: Gourmet Belgian Hot Chocolate
directions: Start off by mixing together the milk and the vanilla bean in a saucepan.
Heat the milk on medium flame.
Do so till you see the appearance of bubbles around the edge of the saucepan.
Reduce flame.
Add the chocolate pieces.
Whisk occasionally, until melted.
Remove from flame.
Remove the vanilla bean.
You may rinse, dry and save the vanilla bean to use later on in another recipe.
If the milk is too thick, add some more milk to thin it.
Just before serving, whisk the milk vigorously to create alot of foam.
Serve with a smile!

similar.name: Rich German Chocolate Cake With a Cherry Shooter or Martini

name: Iced Coffee Polish Style
directions: Combine sugar and coffee.
Chill several hours.
Place 1/4 cup ice cream in each of 4 glasses.
Add 1 1/2 ounces vodka to each glass.
Divide the coffee mixture among the glasses and serve topped with whipped cream.

name: Hot Vodka with Honey (Krupnik)
directions: Heat sugar in 2 Tbs of water until it dissolves, then stir in the boiling water.
Add vanilla bean, nutmeg, cloves, cinnamon stick, peppercorns, and allspice berries.
Bring to a boil, cover and simmer for 5 minutes.
Strain the caramel mixture and return to the pan.
Stir in honey and orange rind and heat, stirring, until the honey has completely dissolved.
Bring to a boil.
Remove the pan from the heat and gradually stir vodka.
Serve hot or cold.

similar.name: Polish Krupnik - Old Fashioned Honey and Spice Liqueur

name: Lemon Fizz Mocktail
directions: Combine apple and lemon juices.
Fill glasses halfway with soda and top off with the juice mixture.
Garnish with a strip of lemon peel.
Enjoy!

name: Alcatraz Wheat Beer
directions: Make a yeast starter two days beforehand.
Mash the three pounds of malt a la Miller.
Boil for one hour, adding 1-1/2 ounces hops at the start, 1/2 ounce at 30 minutes, and 1/2 ounce at 5 minutes.
Cool and pitch yeast.
Ferment.
Bottle.
I primed half the batch (5 gal) with 1/3 cup corn sugar and the other half with 1/2 cup clover honey.
After two weeks, the beer was great.
The beer primed with honey, however, was way too carbonated.
All you can taste is bubbles.

similar.name: Original German Wheat Beer Cola Mixed Drink (Cola Weizen)
Berliner Weisse Mit Schuss (Berlin Style German Beer)
Jerry’s Country Coffee for Hard Times

name: Homemade Drambuie
directions: Combine all ingredients in a tightly covered container.
Shake several times for the first 24 hours.
After 24 hours, remove lemon zest.
Cover and let stand for 2 weeks in a cool, dark place shaking gently every other day.
Strain through a sieve to remove the angelica and fennel seeds.
cover and let stand in a cool dark place for several months (preferably 6 months).
Pour clear liquid into carafe for drinking enjoyment.
Cloudy dregs may be saved for cooking.

name: Dog's Nose
directions: Pour the gin into a beer mug and then add the beer.

similar.name: Berliner Weisse Mit Schuss (Berlin Style German Beer)

name: Thyme Liqueur
directions: Place the thyme in a bottle that holds more than 1 quart.
Add the vodka and let stand for 5 weeks in a dark cupboard, shaking every 2-3 days.
Add the sugar and shake well.
Let stand for another week, shaking once every 2 days.
The sugar should be dissolved by the end of the week.
Pass the liqueur through a dampened coffee filter and transfer to a 1-quart bottle.
Add the branch of fresh thyme and seal.
Store in a dark, cool place.
Will keep for more than 1 year.

similar.name: Louisa: Greek Lemon Verbena Herbal Tea
Spiced Thyme and Lime Iced Tea

name: Harry Potter's Butterbeer
directions: In a medium bowl cream together the ice cream, butter, sugar and spices.
Re-freeze mixture.
In a medium pot heat apple cider to just Hot.
Place 6 tall or 12 short glasses on counter.
Scoop equal amounts of ice cream mixture into each glass.
Pour hot cider on top and serve with spoons for stirring.

name: Harry Potter's Pumpkin Juice Mix
directions: Measure all ingredients into a clean dry jar.
Stir or shake well.
To serve: 2-3 heaping tsp with 1 mug hot water.
Serve hot or chilled.

name: Harry Potter's Dragon Snot
directions: Place sherbert in a glass and pour on sprite or 7-Up.
Serve with a spoon.

name: Harry Potter's Muggle Butterbeer
directions: Place soda in a microwave safe bowl and heat for about 30 seconds on high.
Remove and set aside.
Melt butter in microwave.
Pour warm soda, butter and honey in a blender and pulse until well combined.
Serve warm.

name: Merry's Mulled Cider
directions: Place the cider in a large pot.
Place the cloves and allspice in a cheesecloth spice bag.
Add the spice bag, sugar and salt to the cider and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer for 15 minutes.
Remove spice bag.
Pour Mulled Cider into 12 mugs, adding one cinnamon stick and one slice lemon to each.
Serve hot.

name: Mocha Affagato
directions: Place a pinch of powdered chocolate in an extra large coffee mug or coffee bowl.
Add a shot of freshly brewed espresso.
Add two scoops of vanilla ice cream.
Dust lightly with chocolate, a la cappuccino.
Allow ice cream to just start to melt and chill the coffee.
Enjoy.

name: Classic Caramel Macchiato
directions: Place caramel syrup in the bottom of cup.
Top with freshly prepared hot espresso.
Spoon very small amount of foamed milk on top of espresso.
Avoid getting any milk into espresso if you like it dry.
You can add just a wee bit of milk otherwise, but not a lot (or it won't be a macchiato anymore!).
If desired, you can top this with whipped cream, and a little bit of warmed caramel topping (squirted from a squeeze bottle is best).
Serve at once.

name: Lemon cream liqueur
directions: Put lemon peels (without white pith) in a jar with the alcohol, leave them to soak for 24 hours shaking the jar a couple of times.
Boil milk and sugar for 25 minutes, leave to cool.
Strain the milk, add strained alcohol, vanilla and whiskey, mix well and put in a bottle.
Keep in the freezer.
Variation: use tangerines instead of lemons.

name: Radler--Wonderful German Beer/Lemonade Concoction
directions: Prepare lemonade as directed on label.
Mix prepared lemonade and beer together in a 1:1 ratio.

similar.name: Berliner Weisse Mit Schuss (Berlin Style German Beer)
Original German Wheat Beer Cola Mixed Drink (Cola Weizen)

name: Pernod (pastis) Classique
directions: Pour the Pernod in a tall glass.
Add the water, and ice cubes.
Stir, then-- Sit back, relax, and enjoy life!

name: Black Currant Liqueur, Liqueur De Cassis
directions: Put the bunches of black currants, with their leaves, in a wide mouthed glass jar or a stoneware bottle.
Pour in the alcohol, cover tightly and let macerate for about two months.
Drain the flavored alcohol from the fruit.
Mix the SUGAR and WATER, and bring just to a boil.
Cool this syrup and add it to the alcohol.
Bottle and cork.
Keep the bottles of liqueur for one month before opening.
To make about 2 quarts.
Je Sais Faire Les Conserves.

name: The Green Fairy
directions: Shake thouroughly and strain into a martini glass.
This would be great for Halloween it has a misty green color.

name: Holiday Hot Buttered Rum
directions: Beat softened butter until smooth& creamy.
Add softened ice cream and beat in well.
Add brown sugar and beat until creamy and well incorporated.
Place mix in freezer in plastic containers of desired size and store until needed.
When ready to serve, place at least 3 tblspns of mixture in a large coffee mug; add boiling water almost to the top,stir; add white or dark rum, stir again and top with freshly grated nutmeg.
Hint: For non-drinkers (including kids) omit rum and add about 1 tsp of rum extract.
Just as tasty, but is non-alcoholic.
So creamy, warm and wonderful.

similar.name: Hot Buttered Rum from Saveur Magazine

name: Honey & Almond Spanish Hot Chocolate
directions: In a saucepan, whisk together cocoa, cornstarch and water.
Whisk in the honey and gradually add milk, vanilla and almond extracts, whisking constantly over medium heat until smooth and slightly thickened.

name: Portuguese Daisy (cocktail)
directions: Combine all drink ingredients in a cocktail shaker half-filled with ice cubes; shake well and pour into an old-fashioned glass.
Garnish with a lemon wedge.
*Superfinesugar is also known as caster sugar in Great Britain.
If you absolutely can't find it, you can make your own by pulsing regular granulated sugar in a blender.

name: Tipperary
directions: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass and stir well.
Strain into a cocktail glass.

name: Dad's Tia Maria
directions: Steep 1 3/4 ounces of instant coffee in 4 cups of boiling water.
Make a syrup by putting 6 cups of sugar in 5 cups of boiling water- allow to cool- then add 2 ounces of pure vanilla extract.
Mix syrup with coffee mixture then add 1 3/4 pints of 190 proof ethyl alcohol (Everclear).
Let cool and enjoy!

name: Green Beer  "slàinte!"
directions: In a chilled pint size glass pour beer add food coloring and mix inches Enjoy!
"Slàinte!" (pronounced "slawn-cha"),.
As the Irish would say. The traditional toast is the Irish equivalent of cheers and means "good health" in Gaelic.

similar.name: Berliner Weisse Mit Schuss (Berlin Style German Beer)

name: Violet Syrup
directions: Select only the freshest and most unblemished violets. Place violet petals in a deep bowl and pour the boiling water over them. Weigh down with a heavy dish to keep them submerged. Place the bowl in a draft-free place at room temperature for 24 hours.

Line a colander with layers of rinsed cheesecloth(I use a clean terrycloth towel) and place over a bowl. Pour violets and liquid into colander, squeezing out juice from the violets; discard the violets.
Place sugar, lemon juice and water in a saucepan and boil into a very thick syrup.

near the candy stage. Add violet water and bring to a rolling boil. Boil 10 minutes or until thickened. Pour into sterile bottles. Allow to cool, then seal and refrigerate. Serve with ice water or club soda.
VARIATION: Substitute 4 cups fragrant rose petals and add 1 cinnamon stick per bottle of syrup. Yield: 2 quarts.

name: Andreas Viestad's Mock Aquavit
directions: Open the vodka bottle, add all the remaining ingredients, and cover tightly.

Let stand in a cool, dark place for 2 to 3 weeks, shaking every 3 or 4 days – how long you let it sit depends on how strong you want it, so taste it after 2 weeks to see if you want to let it go longer.
When it’s as strong as you’d like, strain the solids through a sieve and discard them, then transfer the aquavit back into the bottle.

To serve:  place your aquavit in the freezer until chilled and you can also place your 1 ounce tall glasses in the refrigerator until chilled – then serve (ask your Scandinavian friends about toasting practices with aquavit, as it can be rather complicated).
Note:  some people prefer non-potato vodka – use whatever you like.

similar.name: Polish Krupnik - Old Fashioned Honey and Spice Liqueur

name: Stoli Siberian
directions: In a shaker filled with ice, combine vodka, grapefruit juice and cranberry juice.
Strain and serve over ice garnished with a lime slice and a cherry.

name: Garlic Rosemary Mashed Potato Martini
directions: Skewer the olive on the rosemary stem. Combine vodka and vermouth with 6 ice cubes in a shaker. Shake very well. Strain and serve in a chilled cocktail glass. Garnish with the skewered olive.

name: Hot Chocolate
directions: Melt the chocolate in a saucepan over low heat, or in the microwave. When the chocolate has melted, add the corn starch and stir well. Be careful with the cornstarch, if you add too much you will get pudding!
In another saucepan bring milk to a boil and pour in the chocolate mixture. Bring back to a boil for 2 minutes to make it thicken.
Pour into cups. Serve it with whipping cream.
Tip:
The better the quality of the chocolate you use, the better your hot chocolate will taste. You can use white, milk or dark chocolate for this recipe.

name: Sima - Finnish Mead
directions: Wash the lemons and peel them thinly. Remove the pith. Slice the lemons and place them with the peel and sugar in a sufficiently large vessel.

Bring half of the water to the boil and pour it over the lemons, peel, honey or molasses and sugar. Stir and leave to stand covered for a while. Add the rest of the water cold. When the liquid is lukewarm add the yeast.

Keep the mead at room temperature until it starts to ferment, i.e. about one day. Put a couple of raisins and 1 tsp of sugar into clean bottles, and strain the mead into the bottles. Loosely cork the bottles and store them in a cool place. The mead is ready when the raisins rise to the surface.

name: Eggedosis
directions: Beat the egg yolks and whites with an electric mixer until thick and lemon-colored.  Add the sugar gradually, continuing to beat.
Use sherbet glasses, and place 1 tsp of brandy in each.  Gently spoon the egg mixture over this.

name: Mulled Wine a La Kolibri
directions: Crush all the spices to release the aromas. Mix them with the vodka and port wine.
Wash the lemon and the oranges (I used blood oranges) very well, and peel them. Add peel to the mixture. Slice a thick slice of both fruits and put them in too.
Peel ginger and chop into bitesize pieces, add the pieces in the liqueur.
Smell the mixture but resist temptation and put it into an airtight container and to the frige overnigth, or 24 hours if possible.
Next day, drain the spices from the liqueur, and mix it with the sugar and the wine. Stir until sugar is dissolved.

Now's the good part - tasting. I've put quite a lot sugar in because I like my mulled wine sweet, but make yours according to your preference. If it's too sweet, add more wine, and if it's too tart, add more sugar.

Heat up only the amount you're going to drink, and you can keep the rest until it's needed. Microwave is really good for heating up mulled wine, but if you heat it up in a pan make sure it doesn't boil.
Serve with raisins and almond chips.

similar.name: Polish Krupnik - Old Fashioned Honey and Spice Liqueur

name: White Tiger's Milk
directions: Pour all of the liqueurs into the milk.
Fold in the egg and sweeten to taste,.
Serve cold with nutmeg sprinkled on top.

Note: The original version also call's for a 1/2 teaspoons aromatic tincture. This recipe was given to my grandmother as a young woman. She had no idea what it was and no one else seemed to either. So, she never used it in the recipe, but did keep the recipe in tact hoping someone could tell her what it was. Another version I have seen also calls for 1/2 tsp Angostura Bitters and.
2 oz Simple Syrup.

name: The Gossip’s Bowl
directions: Warm together the ale and wine.
Stir in the nutmeg and sugar.
Float the crab apples in the warm ale/wine.

name: Hot Chocolate With Rum
directions: In a medium saucepan, combine the milk with the cream and rum and bring to a simmer over moderate heat. Remove the pan from the heat, add the chocolate and let stand until melted.
Whisk until smooth, pour into warmed mugs and serve.

name: Bartender's Sugar Syrup
directions: Put 4 tablespoons of water and 4 tablespoons of caster sugar into a small saucepan and stir over a low heat until the sugar has dissolved.
Bring to the boil, then continue to boil without stirring, for 1-2 minutes.
Cool then refrigerate in a covered container and keep for no longer than 2 weeks.

name: Sham-Pagne Cocktail
directions: Empty the pear halves and syrup into a blender, add the elderflower cordial and lemon juice then 'whizz' ingredients together to a smooth puree.
Pour 2 fluid ounces puree into the bottom of each glass.
Top glasses up with tonic water (watch it fizz!)and give the contents of each glass a quick stir; serve with a cocktail cherry.
NOTE: you can make the puree in advance and leave it in the fridge until you're ready to use it; the longer you leave the puree, the more the flavours will develop.

name: Lavender Blue Tea
directions: Begin to preparing your teapot for brewing. Boil a kettle of water and pour into the teapot. Cover with lid and let set for 4-5 minutes.
In the meantime, boil another kettle of water. Discard the water from the teapot; cool and save for watering your houseplants.
Add the lavender blossoms and Early Grey loose tea to the tea strainer. Pour the newly boiled water into the teapot. Cover teapot with tea cosy or cotton towel and let steep 5 minutes.
After steeping, for each teacup add appproximately 1/4-1/3 teaspoon heavy cream. Pour the tea and serve with 1-2 sugar cubes (on the saucer) per person.
Teatime! Don't forget the sweets!
Please note that proportions are estimated. You may have to fiddle around with the measurements to get a strength  and balance of flavor that you prefer.

similar.name: Louisa: Greek Lemon Verbena Herbal Tea

name: Polish Mixed Fruit Tea
directions: Crush in mortar.
Mix and store in container.
To make tea, steep 1 spoonful 10 minutes.

name: Bicerin
directions: At least 15 minutes before making bicerin, place stainless steel cocktail shaker or jar in the freezer.
Fill 2 water goblets or Irish coffee mugs with hot tap water.
In a small saucepan, combine chocolate with 2/3 cup water and set over medium heat.  Simmer, stirring occasionally, until chocolate coats spoon, about 10 minutes.
Add sugar to taste and shut off heat.
Empty glasses and wipe dry.
Remove shaker or jar from freezer, add cream, and shake vigorously 1 minute.
Make espresso.
To each glass, add a shot of espresso and 1/3 cup chocolate, and carefully spoon 1/3 cup cream over top.  Serve immediately.

name: Limoncello
directions: Peel the lemons - (be sure to peel only the yellow part because the white is bitter) - put them into a jar together with the alcohol, the water and the sugar.
Give it a good stir and close the jar.
Keep the mix into the jar for about 5 days and don't forget to give it a stir on day 3.
Then, strain the mixture into a bottle and store it - well closed - into the fridge for about a week.
And then -- enjoy your limoncello!

name: Viagra Falls
directions: Shake the absinthe, apple brandy and  orange bitters with ice, then strain into a traditional martini cocktail glass.
Heat a large ring of orange peel over an open flame until the oil of orange is released, then allow the oil to drip into the glass.
Garnish with a ring of orange peel for a great show!

name: Polish Fire Vodka (Krupnik) (Spiced Honey Vodka)
directions: In a large saucepan, combine all ingredients except for vodka and bring to a boil.
Cover and reduce heat to a simmer; simmer for 5 minutes.
Add the vodka, and when heated through, sreve immediately in Irish coffee cups serve chilled later in juice glasses (can also be reheated for serving later).

similar.name: Polish Krupnik - Old Fashioned Honey and Spice Liqueur

name: Hot Mocha
directions: Heat milk and chocolate in a heavy saucepan, stirring until smooth.
Add sugar and salt; bring to a boil, turn heat back and let simmer for 5 minutes, stirring.
Add vanilla, coffee, and light cream.
Serve hot.

name: Sacred Pakistani Chai Tea Recipe
directions: Fill large pan 80% with water and bring to rapid boil.
add tea . boil two minutes.
add rounded tbs. chai masala, cinnamon, ginger, cardamom. boil 5-10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
add sugar. bring to soft boil, adjust heat to medium ( do not allow to boil over!).
add milk. allow to heat just under boiling. remove from heat. cool two minutes.
pour into cups using tea strainer. serve very hot.

similar.name: Louisa: Greek Lemon Verbena Herbal Tea

name: Strawberry  Adult Drink!!!
directions: Mix all ingredients together.
Enjoy!

name: Greek Mama
directions: Shake with ice and strain pouring into a martini glass.

name: Greek Tycoon
directions: Combine and pour into a Brandy snifter.

similar.name: Louisa: Greek Lemon Verbena Herbal Tea

name: Greek Sex on the Beach
directions: Mix and serve over ice.
Garnish with lime.

similar.name: Sex on the Beach With a Dead German Rockstar

name: Grizzly Bear
directions: Stir all ingredients except milk in a glass with ice.
Top with milk.
Serve.

name: Oatmeal Cookie
directions: In a shot glass, layer butterscotch schnapps, Irish cream and Jagermeister. Float cinnamon schnapps on top.
Drink!

name: French Kiss
directions: Shake all ingredients with ice & strain into a martini glass.
Garnish with a Hershey's Kiss or a raspberry.

similar.name: Sex on the Beach With a Dead German Rockstar

name: Icelandic Hot Shot
directions: Pour 1 ounce Galliano Liqueur in cordial glass.
Pour one ounce coffee machine dripped espresso slowly so as not to mix Galliano and espresso.
Any coffee can be used, espresso is suggested which should be resting on top of the Galliano.
Top off with a squirt of whipped cream then shoot it down in one gulp.

name: Italian Screwdriver
directions: In a tall glass, add all ingredients except ice.
Stir.
Add ice to glass to fill.

name: Saint Moritz Martini
directions: Add ingredients to a shaker filled with ice.
Shake & strain into 5 ounce martini glass.

name: Russian Milk Shake
directions: Place ice cream and milk in a blender with syrup of choice and blend till smooth. Drink immediately!

name: Hot Chocolate Zabaglione
directions: Half fill a medium saucepan with water and bring to the simmering point.
Place the egg yolks and sugar in a heatproof bowl and whisk until the mixture is pale and all the sugar is dissolved.
Add the cocoa and Marsala, then place the bowl over the simmering water. Whisk until the consistency of the mixture is smooth, thick and frothy.
Pour quickly into tall heatproof glasses, dust lightly with cocoa or confectioner's sugar, serve immediately with almond cookies.

name: Herbal Eau-De-Vie
directions: Pour out some of the eau-de-vie. Drink it or put it aside!
Put your herb of choice into the bottle.
Close tightly and put it in a cool dark place.
Allow to steep for at least 30 days.
Enjoy!

name: Puccini
directions: Squeeze mandarin orange and pass its juice through a sieve.
Pour it into a flûte.
Just before serving add sparkling wine.
Garnish with orange zest.

name: Chambord Truffle
directions: Pour all ingredients into a shaker over ice.
Chill.
Strain into shot glass.

name: Liqueur à La Sauge (Homemade Sage Liqueur)
directions: Put the leaves and flowers into a bottle or jar and add the eau-de-vie.
Cover and leave in a warm place for eight days.
On the ninth day, strain the liqueur into another clean jar.
Over low heat, dissolve the sugar in the water, then bring it up to a boil.
Cool the syrup and add it to the eau-de-vie.
Seal the bottle and store in a cool, dark place or drink immediately.
To serve: Serve in a small glass as an apéritif or digestive.

similar.name: Black Currant Liqueur, Liqueur De Cassis

name: Almond French Toast
directions: Put oven rack in middle position and preheat to 275ºF.
Whisk together eggs, half and half, sugar, vanilla and salt in a large shallow dish until well combined.
Spread almonds on a large plate.
Soak 4 slices of bread in the egg mixture, turning over once, until saturated.
Working with 1 slice at a time, remove the bread, letting the excess egg mixture drip off, then dredge in almonds to coat both sides, gently pressing to help adhere.
Transfer to a plate or wax paper. Repeat procedure with remaining 4 slices.
Heat 1 1/2 Tbs butter in a 12-inch heavy skillet over medium heat until foam subsides, then cook 4 bread slices, turning over once, until almonds and bread are golden brown, 5-6 minute.
Add the remaining 1 1/2 Tbs butter and cook the remaining 4 slices in the same manner. Transfer the cooked French toast to a baking sheet and keep warm in the oven while cooking the last 4 pieces.

similar.name: Seize the Day Cafe Au Lait

name: Iced Tea With Grenadine
directions: In a large container, mix together the tea, lemon juice, and grenadine. Pour over ice when ready to serve.

name: Vermouth Cassis
directions: Fill a highball glass with ice.
Pour vermouth and creme de cassis over ice and fill with club soda.
Stir gently.

name: Blackjack
directions: Shake with ice and pour into cocktail glass.

name: Christmas Shot
directions: Pour half shot Chambord first, then slowly pour half shot Midori over the back of a spoon into glass.

Okay, I just made this drink.  It is a bit to strong for my taste.  I think it must be the Midori, as I love Chambord.  So if you don't like your drinks strong (like me) you might want to try another recipe.

name: Black Cat
directions: Combine in a shaker with a cup of ice.
Shake well and strain into a chilled martini glass.

name: And the Beet Goes On
directions: Wash and trim vegetables.
Juice carrots and celery first, then juice beet.
Stir and drink! Enjoy!

similar.name: Beet and Ginger Kvass Treat Your Liver Good!

name: Chamomile Herb Tea
directions: Rinse the flowers with cool water.
Warm your tea pot with boiling water.
Add the apple slices to the pot and mash them with a wooden spoon.
Add the chamomile flowers and pour in the 2 cups boiling water.
Cover and steep for 3-5 minutes.
Strain the tea into two cups.
Add honey to taste.
Drink and enjoy!

similar.name: Louisa: Greek Lemon Verbena Herbal Tea

name: Iced Mocha Cafe
directions: Pour the hot coffee into a heatproof 10-ounce glass.
Add the ice cubes and stir until coffee is chilled.
Add the ice cream and top with the whipped cream.
Taste and add sugar, if desired.
Serve with a long-handled spoon and a straw.

name: 5 Star Hot Cocoa Mix
directions: Combine all the dry ingredients in a large bowl and mix well.
Take the bittersweet chocolate and roughly chop it up to give a chunky but consistent size.
Mix well with a wooden spoon and then store in a dry airtight container.
To make the hot chocolate, simply add 1 cup of water per 1/2 cup of hot cocoa mix.
Heat in a small saucepan over low heat, stirring with a whisk.
Heat until hot, but do not boil, about 6 to 7 minutes.
Serve with marshmallows.

name: Poppy Seed Milk
directions: Place the seeds in a 1 qt or 2 qt measuring cup, or a medium bowl.  Add enough hot water to cover, about 3 cups. Allow the seeds to steep in the water for 10 minutes. A little longer shouldn't hurt.
Transfer teh seeds and water to a working blender. Blend at high speed until very smooth, about 3 to 4 minutes. Allow to cool for 5 minutes.
Meanwhile, arrange several layers of overlapping cheese cloth near a clan medium bowl.  Spoon mixture into the cheesecloth, twist the cloth closed and squeeze over the new bowl.
Discard the pulp from inside the cheesecloth and repeat procedure with remainging mixture in the blender.  Whisk the sugar and extract into the milk, then refrigerate until chilled.

name: Amaretto
directions: Combine water and sugars in a very large saucepan over medium heat.
Heat until the mixture is boiling and all sugar is dissolved.
Reduce heat to low and continue to simmer for approximately 2 hours until mixture is thickened and syrupy and lightly coats a spoon.
Remove pan from heat and allow to cool for 10 minutes.
Add vodka and extracts and stir well.
Pour into a bottle and seal.

name: Cucumber Martini !
directions: Stir Vermouth and Hendrick's Gin over ice cubes in mixing glass; strain into martini glass.
Serve with cucumber slice.
Sip it, Savor it, & Enjoy it!

name: Hendrick's Gin and Tonic
directions: Pour Hendrick's Gin into highball glass over ice cubes and fill with tonic water; stir.
Garnish with Cucumber spear.

name: Cupid's Cosmo
directions: Place all in a shaker with lots of ice and shake well.
Pour into a chilled martini glass.
Garnish with and orange rind.

name: Hot Buttered Rum from Saveur Magazine
directions: Beat butter and sugar in a bowl with an electric mixer until fluffy.
Add spices and salt and beat again to combine.
Chill buttered rum base until ready to use, up to 2 weeks.
To make one hot buttered rum, put 2 tablespoons of base and 1 1/2 oz. rum into an 8 oz. mug and fill with boiling water.
Stir to melt and mix; serve at once.

name: Hot Irish Whiskey
directions: Mix whiskey and sugar in pitcher to dissolve sugar.
Divide among 6 heat-proof glasses.
Stud each lemon slice with 4 cloves.
Place 1 slice in each glass.
Pour 1/2 cup hot water into each; stir to blend.

name: Mulsum or Honeyed Wine
directions: Warm 1/2 cup clear honey.
Add honey to medium-dry white wine.
Chill before serving.
Serve.
Roman Togas optional. Latin not needed.

name: Swiss Absinthe Warmer  Louche Effect!
directions: Put a sugar cube on a special absinthe spoon and lay it on the rim of the glass.
Pour absinthe over the sugar cube and into the glass.
Enlighten the sugar and let it burn until it shows bubbles or starts to caramelize.
Then stir the sugar in the glass.
At once add ice water so the alcohol inside the glass doesn't ignite. Then add water to taste.

name: Lady Godivas' Hot Toddy
directions: Put the sugar, bitters, lemon juice, and clove-studded lemon slice in the glass. Add the Cognac and pour in the boiling water. Stir to dissolve the sugar and sprinkle with ground nutmeg. Now, that's a toddy!

name: Irish Quaalude (Shooter)
directions: Combine all ingredients in shaker with ice.
Vigorously shake and strain.

name: The Dirty Leprechaun
directions: Layer ingredients in shot glass using back of spoon to separate layers.
Ingredients can be combined in a shaker with ice and strained into shot glass.

name: Anise Milk
directions: Put the milk over medium heat, preferably in a pan with a thick bottom (and high enough, the milk will rise when starting to boil).

Mix in the crushed anise, crushed cardamom and honey using a whisk. Beat the milk using the whisk to give it a layer of foam. Then let it stand (occasionally stirring it with the whisk) until it boils.
Pour the milk into the cup through a strainer and let it cool a bit (it should still be hot, but not so hot that you'll burn your tongue, otherwise you won't taste much).

name: Shamrock Shooter
directions: Stir all ingredients together.
Pour over ice in a highball glass, or serve in a shot glass as a shooter.
Garnish with a shamrock leaf.

name: Godiva Peppermint Patty
directions: Pour hot chocolate into a brandy snifter.
Add Rumple Minze and Godiva liqueurs.  Stir.
Top with whipped cream.

similar.name: Rich German Chocolate Cake With a Cherry Shooter or Martini

name: Peanut Butter Cup
directions: Blend all ingredients over ice.
Can be strained and served up or on the rocks.

similar.name: Seize the Day Cafe Au Lait

name: Flavored Vodkas
directions: To make a lemon flavored vodka (Limonnaya):
Make sure no white is left on the rind, as it will make the taste bitter.
Add the zest to the vodka and infuse for at least 4 hours, but no more than 12 at room temperature. Strain and chill.
To make Buffalo Grass Vodka (Zubrovka):
Add the buffalo grass to the vodka and infuse for at least 8 hours. Remove all but two blades of the buffalo grass. Chill.
To make Pepper Vodka (Pertzovka):
Add the peppercorns to the vodka and infuse for at least 8 hours, 3 days maximum. Strain and chill.
To make Coriander Vodka (Koriandrovaya):
Add the coriander seeds to the vodka and infuse for at least 6 hours, maximum 24 hours. Strain and chill.
To make Anise Vodka(Anisovaya):
Add the aniseed to the vodka and infuse for 3 to 5 hours. Strain and chill.

name: Hard to Get
directions: Pour chilled  Fusion in a rocks glass.
Sink raspberry liqueur and float apple pucker (see tip in description if you need help on sinking and floating).
Enjoy! ;).

name: Sauerkraut Juice Cocktail
directions: Combine ingredients in order listed.
Serve cold in cocktail glasses.

name: Negus
directions: Gently heat all the ingredients in a small saucepan.
Strain into a heat-proof goblet when hot.

name: Irish Brogue
directions: Fill a rocks glass with ice. Add ingredients and stir.

name: Tom and Jerry's
directions: Take 4 mugs and fill 1/3 full with eggnog.
Heat water, brandy and rum to boiling point.  Mix then pour equal parts into mugs.
Sprinkle drinks with nutmeg, put in cinnamon sticks and serve.

similar.name: Hot Buttered Rum from Saveur Magazine

name: The Duke of Bedford
directions: Fill a julep or highball glass with crushed ice.
In a cocktail shaker, combine sherry, mint leaves, orange soda, sugar and bitters and shake well.
Strain over ice and garnish with mint sprig and cucumber slice.

name: English Cucumber Vodka Infusion
directions: Use a large, clean glass container (at least 1.5 liter) with an air tight lid.
Save the original Vodka bottle to strain the infused mixture into later.
Place the cucumber in a large glass container, add the vodka and cap tightly.
Let stand at room temperature for 3 weeks, shaking gently every couple of days if needed.
Taste for the preferred flavour intensity.  If you want it stronger, let it sit for up to another week.  If you let it sit longer it will go bitter.
Strain the infused Vodka into the original bottle.
Cap tightly, label and refrigerate until ready to serve.

name: Gvc Cocktail Using Ginger Wine.
directions: Shake all and strain into chilled cocktail glass.
Garnish with lime wheel.
Serve very cold.

similar.name: Ginger Martini from the Odeon in TriBeCa

name: Framboise Champagne Cocktail
directions: Place one sugar cube in each of 8 glasses, add a capful or 2 teaspoons of liqueur to each glass.
Fill each glass with well chilled champagne.
Serve immediately.

name: French 75
directions: Half-fill a 14-ounce collins glass with crushed ice. Pour in the gin and lemon juice and stir in the sugar. Pour in the champagne.

name: Elderflower Cordial
directions: Shake elderflowers to expel any insects, and then place in large bowl.
Put sugar into a pan with the water and bring to boil, stirring until sugar has dissolved.
While the sugar syrup is heating, pare zest of lemons off in wide strips and toss into bowl with elderflowers. Slice lemons, discard the ends, and add slices to bowl.
Pour over boiling syrup, and stir in the citric acid. Cover with a cloth and leave at room temp for 24 hours.
Next day, strain cordial through a sieve lined with muslin (or a new J-Cloth rinsed out in boiling water), and pour into thoroughly cleaned glass or plastic bottles.
Screw on lids and it's ready to use.
TO SERVE: Dilute cordial to taste with fizzy water, and serve over ice.

name: Cilantro Sparkling Wine Cooler
directions: Tie the cilantro to secure in place before bruising the leaves and stems with the flat blade of a knife. This helps extract the maximum flavour.
In a pitcher, infuse the cilantro with the wine for 10 minutes or so. Sharpen with lime juice, stir well and pour into six wine glasses.

name: Icy Caramel Cappuccino
directions: Place fat free half & half, coffee packet(Cappuccino Classics  Suprema, Suisse Mocha, Amaretto Italia or French Vanilla), ice cream topping and ice cream in 5-cup blender container. Cover; blend, scraping sides occasionally, until smooth (50 to 60 seconds).
Pour into individual glasses. Serve with dollop of whipped cream; sprinkle with chopped candy.

name: Tivoli by Night
directions: Place cherry liqueur, pineapple juice and kahlua in a shaker with ice cubes.
Strain into a glass, serve garnished with maraschino cherry and lemon slice.

name: Black Currant Schnapps - Sweden
directions: Add the black currants and the mint to an empty, clean bottle.
Add the sugar cubes and pour in the ABSOLUT VODKA.
Leave it four about one week before serving.

similar.name: Black Currant Liqueur, Liqueur De Cassis

name: Danish Coffee
directions: Combine all the ingredients in a large saucepan.
Cover and keep on low heat for about 2 hours.
Drain & Serve in large mugs.

similar.name: Swedish Spiced Cardamom Coffee With Chantilly Whipped Cream

name: Alexander Cocktail
directions: In a blender, mix until smooth the cream, cognac,crème de cacao, sugar and lime zest.
Place a spoonful of crushed ice into each of 6 shallow cocktail or champagne glasses.
Pour the cream mixture over the ice and sprinkle with ground cinnamon.
Serve immediately garnished with cinnamon sticks as stirrers.

name: Parisian Passion
directions: Shake all the ingredients, except the Champagne, together in a cocktail shaker. Strain, over chilled Champagne, into a Champagne saucer glass.

name: Bicerin
directions: At least 15 minutes before making the bicerin, place stainless steel cocktail shaker or jar in the freezer and fill two water goblets or irish coffee mugs with hot tap water.
In a small saucepan combine chocolate with 2/3 cup water and set over medium heat.
Simmer for 10 minutes or until chocolate coats the spoon.
Add sugar to taste and turn off heat.
Empty glasses and wipe dry.
Remove shaker from freezer and add cream.  Shake vigorously for 1 minute.
Make espresso and add a shot to each glass with 1/3 cup chocolate mixture.
Carefully spoon 1/3 cup cream over top and serve immediately.

name: French Daiquiri
directions: MethodIn a cocktail shaker, stir together the sugar with the lime juice until the sugar is dissolved.
Then add brandy and cubed ice and shake hard.
Strain into two martini glasses.

name: Hot Peppermint Puff
directions: Heat milk but do not boil.
Dissolve peppermints & marshmallows in milk.
Whisk until fully blended & frothy.

name: Frappé
directions: In a blender or cocktail shaker, dissolve the coffee in the hot water.
Add the remaining ingredients and blend or.
Shake until very frothy.
Pour into a tall glass and serve immediately.

name: Elderberry Wine
directions: Make a maceration of the flowers into 10 liters of the wine for 30 days - stirring once every 7 days.
Filter out the flowers.
Add the sugar and the alcohol that has been heated to 90 degrees Celsius and stir.
Allow the sediment to settle over the next 3 days.
Place in wine bottles and cork.

name: Nut Wine
directions: Make a mixture of the nuts and alcohol and have it sit for 3 months.
Filter out the nuts.
Add the sugar.
Let mixture settle for another 2 to 3 days.

name: Baby Guinness
directions: Pour the Kahlua into a shot glass.
Whip the Irish cream enough to make it foamy with bubbles.
Carefully spoon the Irish cream on top of the Kahlua.
Cheers!

name: Ankka Juoma (Finn Beer)
directions: Combine all ingredients in large container.
Mix well.
Let stand over night.
Pour into bottles.
Seal.
Yield 11 (12 ounce) servings.

name: Amarina
directions: Shake altogether in shaker and serve over ice.

name: Mint Limonada
directions: Add crushed ice to a rocks glass and muddle the mint sprig.
Add lemon juice, vodka, and limoncello, and stir to combine.
Splash with soda water or 7-UP and serve.

name: Jenna J
directions: Pour all the ingredients on ice in a highball glass.
Squeeze lime above the glass, then put them in the drink.
Serve with long straws.

name: Kurant Blush
directions: Put all ingredients in a shaker with ice and shake well.
Strain 2 times in an iced cocktail glass.
Garnish with red currant.

name: Soviet Sunset Cocktail
directions: Fill a frosty 8 ounce glass with ice cubes.
Pour equal amounts vodka, triple sec and lime juice over the ice.
Fill to the top with club soda and stir.

name: Rosebud
directions: Fill an 8-ounce glass with ice and add grapefruit juice.
In a cocktail shaker, mix vodka, lime juice and Triple Sec. Strain on top of ice and grapefruit juice.
Stir gently.
Sit back, relax and sip.

name: Gogl-Mogl - Good for What Ails Ya!
directions: In a saucepan, add all the ingredients in the order given (except the nutmeg).
Beat constantly over medium heat, using a wire whisk, until well blended.
Reduce heat and continue to beat, whisking constantly until the mixture is very frothy.
Serve immediately with a pinch of grated nutmeg.

similar.name: A Glezel Tei - Yiddish Glass of Tea

name: Normandy Dream
directions: Mix all the ingredients and strain into a serving glass.
Enjoy on the veranda, where you are waiting for the sun to set.

name: French Kiss
directions: Shake ingredients with ice and strain into a martini glass.
Garnish with a Hershey's Kiss or a raspberry.

name: Wheat Germ Posset
directions: Heat milk in a pan to near boiling point.
Remove from heat & stir in other ingredients.
Sprinkle with cinnamon.

name: Citrus Bubbly
directions: Add Absolut Citron and Chambord to a champagne flute.
Fill to the top with champagne.
Garnish with a lemon twist.

name: Sherry Cocktail
directions: Shake vodka, sherry, and orange juice with ice cubes in a cocktail shaker.
Strain into a cocktail glass filled with ice.
Garnish with an orange peel.

name: Portuguese " Secret" Coffee
directions: Into a mug, place the coffee and sugar. Mix in a couple of drops of tepid water and stir until the mixture looks like black cake dough. Keep mixing until it turns white, or yellow, then fill with boiling water.
By oxygenating the coffee, you end up with a creamy yellow drink with foam on top.

name: Liquid Cannoli
directions: Mix the two ingredients together over ice and serve!

name: Passionfruit Champagne Cocktails
directions: Place 5 drops of bitters, or to taste, in bases of 6 glasses.
Place a sugar cube in each glass and pour over the sparkling wine.
Add the passionfruit pulp and serve.

name: Midnight Rose Cocktail
directions: Shake all ingredients in a cocktail shaker & strain into a a glass.

name: Virgin Bell Peppered Mary
directions: Juice the bell peppers, cucumber, carrots,  celery, cilantro(if using), parsley, garlic, and ginger in a juicer.
Stir in the cayenne, salt, and pepper to taste. You may add some tomato juice now, if desired.
Pour into glasses and garnish with celery sticks if you wish.
Serve immediately and enjoy!

name: Roman Coffee
directions: Sugar frost a champagne saucer.
Pour in the Galliano & ignite it, twist the glass around at 45 degree angle to caramelise the sugar.
Once the sugar has caramelised immediately pour in the hot coffee.
Float lightly whipped cream on top to serve.

name: Toblerone
directions: Blend with ice and serve in a tall glass.
Garnish with choc flakes.
To make it look really nice, drizzle chocolate topping down the inside of the glass before pouring in drink.

name: Angel Kiss
directions: Pour the ingredients in the order listed over the back of a spoon.
This will "float" the liqueurs on top of each other to create a beautiful layered effect.
Pour all ingredients into a pony or.
pousse café glass.
*Here's a tip: use a new, clean spoon for.
each ingredient and don't allow the spoon.
to touch the liquid in the glass. And.
pour very, very slowly for the best results.
Yes, this makes it a bit more time consuming, but think of the show you are putting on for your guests. It's a bit of magic and they will appreciate the thoughtfulness.

name: Thyme for French Healing Tea!
directions: Place thyme in tea cup and pour over boiling water to cover. Cover cup and allow to steep for 10 minutes.
Strain and serve, with honey and/or lemon, if you so desire. Enjoy!

similar.name: Spiced Thyme and Lime Iced Tea

name: Irish Creamy Tea
directions: Combine all ingredients in a 360ml mug.

name: Sultan of Swing
directions: Keep the grenadine and the Noilly Prat in the fridge. So they are already cold enough.
Mix first four ingredients.
Fill a Martini glass with ice.
Pour the mixture over the ice and garnish with lemon wedges.
NOTE: I love Noilly Prat and would recommend to use it. If you can't get it, use a very dry vermouth. Use a high quality pomegranate syrup, because it makes this cocktail.

name: Strawberry Jive
directions: Muddle the mint, strawberries and basil leaves in the bottom of a mixing glass with the syrup.
Add the remaining ingredients except the mint sprig garnish and shake well with ice.
Strain into an ice filled rocks glass garnish with mint sprig.

name: Baileys Spiced Frappe
directions: Blend all ingredients with 2-3 ice cubes. Serve in a glass over crushed ice. Garnish with a sprinkle of nutmeg or a cinnamon stick.

name: Mleko Z Miodem - Polish Milk & Honey Bedtime Drink
directions: Put the milk & honey in a saucepan & slowly bring to the boil. Remove from the heat the second it reaches the boil.
Add the butter if you have a cold & serve straight away for a good nights sleep.

name: The Original Shannon Airport Irish Coffee
directions: Take a wine glass and pour enough of the coffee to fill it just over half way.
Add the sugar and stir until it dissolves.
Add the whisky and stir.
Slowly pour a thick layer of double cream, over the back of a teaspoon, on to the top of the coffee so that it floats on top.
Do NOT stir the coffee, but drink it through the cream.

name: Mexican Irish Coffee
directions: Whip the cream with 1 teaspoon sugar until soft peaks form, set aside.
Combine remaining sugar, coffee, whiskey and tequila or Tia Maria.
Pour into mugs and top with lots of whipped cream.

name: Blacksmith
directions: Pour Smithwick's into a pint glass and top up with Guinness.

name: Trojan Horse
directions: Pour Coke into a pint glass and top up slowly with Guinness.

name: Black & Brown
directions: Pour root beer into a pint glass and slowly top up with Guinness.

name: Black & Tan
directions: Pour Harp or Bass into a pint glass and top up with Guinness.

name: Kahlua White Leprechaun
directions: Ingredients should read parts and not ounces; make according to your taste.
Shake well with ice and strain into a rocks or martini glass. Garnish with fresh mint and vanilla bean.

name: Burning Bush
directions: Place lemon wedge in Toddy Glass or Irish coffee glass.
Pour whiskey and honey over lemon.
Fill with water.

name: Pimm's With Lemonade
directions: Combine ice, Pimm's, strawberries, mint and cucumber in a large jug. Slowly add lemonade.
Add a little more ice, if necessary; serve immediately.

name: Blue Crantini
directions: Place all ingredients in cocktail shaker with ice. Shake and strain into a martini glass.

name: Brulot Charentais - Angel's Flames - French Flambé Coffee
directions: You need HEAT RESISTANT earthenware cups, saucers, and a heatproof coaster. You need 4 cls of cognac, but the Zaar recipe editor would not allow me to post that - which is about a 1 jigger measure.
Place the cup in the saucer (it is preferable to put the saucer on a plate or tray).

Fill the coffee cup with cold coffee, up to two centimetres below the top of the cup, then slowly pour 1 cl  of the Cognac onto the coffee (best to use the back of a tea/coffee spoon, so it floats and can catch light).
Pour the remaining 3 cls of Cognac into the saucer, then place 2 – 3  sugar cubes in the cognac.

Light the sugar cubes with a match; the Cognac will slowly ignite, heating the coffee and combining with the sugar to form a Cognac liqueur. (It is important to let the Cognac burn and extinguish naturally; if not, too much alcohol will remain in the mixture, and the special 'alchemy' between the coffee and the Cognac will be lost.).

When the flame has extinguished, wait for a little while for the handle to cool down, place the cup on the coaster. Empty all or part of the contents of the saucer into the cup (depending on personal taste and sweetness required).
Your Brulot Charentais is now ready to drink. Salut!

similar.name: Black Currant Liqueur, Liqueur De Cassis

name: Greek Revolution Shooter
directions: Shake all ingredients with ice.
Strain into a shot glass and serve.

name: Greek Doctor
directions: Shake all ingredients with ice.
Strain into an old-fashion glass filled with ice.

similar.name: Louisa: Greek Lemon Verbena Herbal Tea

name: Tko Shooter
directions: Shake all ingredients with ice.
Strain into a shot glass.

name: Mount Olympus Cocktail
directions: Blend all ingredients together in an ice-filled blender.
Serve in a long stemmed cocktail glass with fruit wedges and a Greek flag.

name: Drunk German
directions: Pour Jagermeister into tall glass.
Add root beer and stir.
Enjoy!

name: Sicilian Holiday
directions: Pour all ingredients into a glass.
Stir and fill with ice.

name: Sex on the Beach With a Dead German Rockstar
directions: Shake all of the ingredients in a cocktail shaker with ice. Strain into a double old fashioned glass or similar to drink as a shooter or just pour it into a Collins glass and drink with a fancy straw! It's good either way.

name: German Carbomb
directions: Pour Heineken into a 16 oz. glass or mug.
Drop a shot of Jager into the beer and drink immediately.

similar.name: Original German Wheat Beer Cola Mixed Drink (Cola Weizen)

name: Rich German Chocolate Cake With a Cherry Shooter or Martini
directions: If you are serving this as a martini it is fun to swirl chocolate syrup around on the glass and to make a cherry flag to go in it.
Shake all ingredients except grenadine in a cocktail shaker with ice.
Strain into glass.
Add grenadine so that it sinks to the bottom.

name: Fiery German Cocktail Shooter
directions: Pour Jager and Schnapps into a shaker with crushed ice and shake to chill. Strain into a shot glass and serve immediately.

name: Palm Beach Fizz Cocktail
directions: Combine first 3 ingredients in a champagne flute & stir well.
Top with champagne.

name: Romanian Orangeade
directions: Squeeze the lemons and oranges. Strain the juice through cheese cloth. (Unless you use a self straining juicer.).
Dissolve the sugar in water, add the lemon and orange juices, pour in a bottle/pitcher with one piece of orange peel and refrigerate. Serve cold after a few hours.

name: Apple Cinnamon Infused Vodka
directions: Slice the apples and toss them into your infusion jar with the cinnamon sticks. Add the bottle of vodka, and let it infuse for a few days.

name: Cucumber Infused Vodka
directions: Place the cucumber in a large glass container, add the vodka, and cap tightly. Let stand at room temperature for 3 weeks, shaking gently every couple of days. Taste for the preferred flavor intensity, allowing it to infuse for up to another week if needed. (Do not allow it to infuse any longer than 2 weeks, or a bitter flavor will result.) Strain the infused vodka into the original bottle. Cap tightly, label, and refrigerate until ready to serve.

name: French Canadian Martini
directions: In a tumbler, add Crown Royal Special Reserve and Grand Marnier.
Stir with ice.
Strain into a martini glass.

name: Red Wine & Coke
directions: Mix 3/4 red wine to 1/4 coke and enjoy  --  .

name: Cliff Hanger Cocktail
directions: Pour alcohol over ice in a glass, top up with lemonade.

name: Red & White Chocolate Mousse Latte
directions: Combine ingredients in a mug, mix well.

name: Secret #43 Cocktail
directions: Fill a shaker with crushed ice.
Add the ingredients in order as above.
Shake for 10 to 12 sec.
Pour over ice in a pina colada glass.

name: Nun's Revenge
directions: In a small bowl, combine 2 tablespoons of the half-and-half with the arrowroot, whisking until smooth.
Place the remaining 1/2 cup of half-and-half in a small saucepan over medium heat.
Bring to a simmer and when the half-and-half begins to bubble around the edges, whisk in the sugar.
Whisk in the arrowroot mixture until the half-and-half thickens slightly, usually less than a minute.
Remove from the heat and quickly whisk in the chocolate until smooth.
Pour into cups, top each with a piece of orange zest, and serve immediately.

name: Spanish Margarita
directions: Combine all ingredients (except lime wedge) in a cocktail shaker filled with ice.
Shake vigorously and pour into a margarita glass with chipped ice.
Garnish with lime wedge.

name: Peary Berry Champers
directions: Combine pear nectar & blueberry liqueur in a large champagne glass, top up with champagne or sparkling wine.

name: Lemony Bastard
directions: Add all ingredients into a shaker and shake away!

name: Limoncello Sunrise
directions: Mix 1 ounce  limoncello  and 3 ounce orange juice.
Top with a dash of grenadine.

name: 43 Pina Colada
directions: Shake with ice in a cocktail shaker.
Pour over ice chilled glass.

name: Caffe Noche by Paul Bassett
directions: Lightly whisk the cream & honey until thickened.
Pour nutella into a warmed martini glass and pour espresso over the top.
Top with the honey cream & hazelnuts, serve with biscotti.

similar.name: Seize the Day Cafe Au Lait

name: Kefi Margarita
directions: Coat the outer rim of a cocktail glass with the crushed fennel and salt, and place the glass into the refrigerator to chill.
Fill a cocktail shaker two-thirds full of ice and add all of the ingredients. Shake for approximately 15 seconds, and strain into the prepared glass.
*note* There is NO 'cooking' time - please disregard that, I couldn't submit until I put a valid number.

name: Amarula Colada
directions: Put all the ingredients into your blender.
Mix well.
Put in high cocktail glases and decorate with canned cherries.

name: Raspberry Beer & Champagne Cocktail
directions: Pour 4 oz of the raspberry beer in a wine glass.
Add 4 oz of champagne.
Celebrate !

name: X-Rated on the Beach
directions: Combine all ingredients & serve over ice.

name: Bicerin
directions: Fill 4 6-ounce heatproof glasses or clear mugs with hot water.
In a medium saucepan, melt chocolate in milk. Whisk until it comes to a boil, then continue whisking for 1 minute.
Empty the glasses and pour 1/4 cup of hot chocolate into each. Over the back of a spoon, gently pour 1/4 cup of coffee on top of hot chocolate. Top off each glass with 1/4 c whipped cream and serve.

name: Lemon Capri
directions: Fill a shaker 3/4 full with ice.  Add Limoncello, coffee, and cream.  Cover and shake until shaker becomes frosty.  Strain into a chilled small glass.

name: Celtic Homemade Hot Buttered Rum
directions: In a small bowl, beat butter together with brown sugar, powdered sugar, nutmeg, and cinnamon.
Beat in the softened ice cream.
Pour the ice cream mixture into a freezer-proof container, seal, and freeze.
When ready to serve, put about 1/3 cup of the ice cream mixture into individual mugs.
Add 1 jigger of rum and 1/2 cup boiling water to each.
Stir well and serve. Add whipped cream if you like.
Variation: Place 2 jiggers of dark rum, a twist of lemon peel, and a cinnamon stick in an ale mug. Fill with boiling hard cider, add a pat of butter, and stir with a cinnamon stick.

similar.name: Hot Buttered Rum from Saveur Magazine

name: Celtic Homemade Mulled Cider
directions: In a large saucepan, combine cider, brown sugar, and nutmeg.
Place cinnamon, allspice, and 1 teaspoon of whole cloves in cheesecloth and tie with string.
Add spice bag to cider mixture; bring to boiling. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for another 5-10 minutes.
Remove spice bag and discard.
Serve cider in mugs with a clove-studded orange wedge in each. Cinnamon sticks also add a decorative touch.

name: Celtic Homemade Atholl Brose
directions: Stir together the oatmeal, honey, and cold water. When they are thoroughly mixed, slowly add the whiskey. Stir briskly until the mixture foams.
Bottle and cork tightly in sterilized containers.
After a day or two remove the cork, strain the mixture, and toast your friends with a hearty "Sláinte agus saol agut!" Health and long life to you!

name: Hot Irish Whiskey (Hot Toddy).
directions: Boil Water. Put a teaspoon into your glass and top up with boiling water to warm glass (the spoon helps prevent breaking). Empty glass and then add 1 oz of whiskey. Add sugar. Fill glass with boiling water and stir. Cut Lemon into quater slices and stick 4/5 cloves into lemon slice. Add to drink and enjoy.

name: Eggnog
directions: Using a mixer, beat the egg yolks in a large bowl for 5 - 10 minutes; they need to be thick and resemble the colour of butter.
A little bit at a time, add in the milk, condensed milk, sugar and vanilla essence.
In a separate bowl, beat the egg whites to stiff peaks.
Slowly fold the egg whites into the rest of the mixture.
Add rum.  Stir gently.
Serve in cups and garnish with ground nutmeg or cinnamon.

name: Holiday Eggnog Cocktail
directions: Combine the eggnog, cognac, bourbon (or rum), and the vanilla extract in a cocktail shaker with ice.
Shake briskly with the ice for 30 seconds; strain into an Old-Fashioned serving glass.
Dust with nutmeg.

name: Café Con Leche
directions: Place coffee in bottom of prewarmed French press. Add boiling water, replace lid (without lowering filter), and allow to brew for 5 minutes.

Meanwhile, place milk in a small saucepan. Bring just to boiling point (the first larger bubble breaking on surface), then reduce heat to barely simmering. Simmer gently for a few minutes, stirring occasionally, until coffee is done.
After the 5 minutes are up, depress plunger on press to strain grounds. Divide coffee evenly between 4 mugs. Add hot milk to each cup in equal amounts to coffee.
Sweeten to taste. Garnish with a dash of cinnamon, if desired.

name: Irish Cowboy
directions: Skae all ingredients in a cocktail shaker with ice, serve in a chilled glass.

name: Spezi (German Orange Cola)
directions: In each glass, mix equal parts orange soda and cola. Squeeze the juice of one lemon wedge into each glass. Serve.

name: Triple-Shot Eggnog
directions: Whisk the egg yolks in a large bowl until they just begin to turn a lighter shade of yellow. Add the sugar and a pinch of salt and whisk until thick and pale yellow.

Whisk in the rum, bourbon, and brandy until well combined and then whisk in the milk, cream, nutmeg, and vanilla until blended. Chill the mixture, covered, for 4 hours or overnight. Keep the egg whites chilled separately in a medium bowl.
Before serving, whip the egg whites to stiff peaks with a hand-held mixer. Fold the whipped egg whites into the chilled yolk mixture.
Serve immediately, sprinkle with a little freshly grated nutmeg, or chill for up to 4 hours. Whisk the eggnog until smooth before serving.

name: Venetian Spritz
directions: Start by pouring the Aperol into a champagne flute.
Next add the prosecco.
Top with sparkling water or soda water and garnish with a half slice of orange.
Stir very gently so as not to dissipate the bubbles.

name: Hippocras
directions: Mix the dry ingredients into the wine, till most of the sugar dissolves.
Let sit for 2 hours, stirring every now and then.

Filter the wine, eliminating all the spices, several times, so as to obtain a clear liquid. I use a cloth towel bag to filter out most of the spice, and then pass the wine in a percolating filter, that works fine.
Bottle and let sit in a cool place for at least a couple of days. Best after a month or two.

name: Celery Soda
directions: Put  the sugar and the water into a medium saucepan on medium-high heat. Stir to mix well. Continue to stir frequently until all of the sugar has dissolved.
Remove from the heat and add the celery seed. Cover and allow to steep for 1 hour.
Strain though a fine mesh strainer into a heat-proof container and place in the refrigerator, uncovered, until completely cool.
Place in an airtight container and store for up to 6 months.
To use, add 2 tablespoons of syrup to 1 cup soda water and stir to combine. Enjoy!

name: Spanish Moss Cocktail
directions: Shake well with cracked ice. Strain into old fashioned glass over 1 ice cube.

name: Chocolate Caliente - Spanish Hot Chocolate Too
directions: Pour the milk into a medium saucepan and add the cornstarch. Whisk to dissolve the cornstarch. Once the cornstarch is dissolved, heat the milk on medium heat just until it boils, then remove from heat.

Add the chocolate  and sugar immediately and begin stirring until the chocolate is completely melted and sugar is disolved. If the milk cools off too fast, place the pan back on the stove on low heat to melt the chocolate.

Place the pan back on the stove on medium low heat, stirring slowly, but constantly. (Do not cook the mixture over high heat because it may cause lumping.) Taste the chocolate for sweetness and add more sugar if necessary. The mixture should thicken quickly. As soon as you see it thicken, remove the pan from the heat so the cornstarch will not thin. Ladle immediately into cups and serve piping hot.
Note: Be sure to use a clean spoon every time you taste the chocolate. Enzymes from your mouth can cause a thickened cornstarch mixture to thin.

name: Chocolate Caliente - Spanish Hot Chocolate
directions: Pour the milk into a medium saucepan and add the cornstarch. Whisk to dissolve the cornstarch. Once the cornstarch is dissolved, heat the milk on medium heat just until it boils, then remove from heat.
Add the chocolate  immediately and begin stirring until the chocolate is completely melted. If the milk cools off too fast, place the pan back on the stove on low heat to melt the chocolate.

Place the pan back on the stove on medium low heat, stirring slowly, but constantly. (Do not cook the mixture over high heat because it may cause lumping.) Taste the chocolate for sweetness and add more sugar if necessary. The mixture should thicken quickly. As soon as you see it thicken, remove the pan from the heat so the cornstarch will not thin. Ladle immediately into cups and serve piping hot.
Note: Be sure to use a clean spoon every time you taste the chocolate. Enzymes from your mouth can cause a thickened cornstarch mixture to thin.

similar.name: Chocolate Caliente - Spanish Hot Chocolate Too

name: Spanish Melonball
directions: Shake midori, malibu, and pineapple juice and strain into a highball glass. Add orange juice.

name: Saucy Senorita
directions: Mix the raspberries and blueberries with the syrup in a cocktail shaker, along with a scoop of ice and the tequila and liqueur.
Shake vigorously and pour into a glass leaving an inch at the top to gently add the cranberry juice.
Garnish with lime and serve.

name: Fruity Sangria
directions: Pour the wine, apple juice, and grapefruit pieces in a large pitcher. Mix well, cover and refrigerate overnight.
The next day, add the orange and lemon slices to the wine. Refrigerate for 3 more hours.
Add the pear and apple to the mixture, then allow to stand for a further 1 hour to absorb the flavor fully. Mix well and serve over ice.

name: Gazpacho Vodka Shots
directions: Place the tomato juice, tomato, cucumber, capsicum, onion, garlic and Worcestershire sauce in the jug of a blender and blend until almost smooth.
Add Tabasco sauce.
Taste and season with salt and pepper.
Divide vodka evenly among shot glasses.
Top with the gazpacho and serve immediately.

name: Mike's Hot Toddy
directions: Squeeze a piece of lemon (1 tablespoon) into a microwave-safe mug.
Add the spoonful of honey, whiskey and hot water.
Microwave until piping hot (maybe 30-45 secs).
Add spices to taste.
Chase that cold away!

name: Chocolat à L'ancienne  (Hot Chocolate)
directions: Heat milk, cream & sugar over low flame.  Bring to simmer but do not let it boil.
Remove from heat and add chocolate and vanilla.  Stir until chocolate melts and mixture is smooth.
Pour into mugs and serve with whipped cream & a sprinkle of powdered cocoa.
Voilà!

name: French Connection
directions: Take out your brandy snifter and add Courvoisier. then  the Grand Marnier.

name: Chambord Truffle Cocktail
directions: Mix together and pour into a shot glass.

name: Ciobar (Thick Hot Chocolate)
directions: Mix the dry ingredients in a thick sauce pan.
Mix in the milk, a tbsp at a time until it becomes smooth and then add the rest of the milk at a faster pace.
Heat mixing all the time for about 5 minutes until thick. Pour into 2 cups and serve steaming hot with a spoon on the side.

name: Softglogg - Soft Glogg (Swedish Soft Drink)
directions: Mix and bring to a boil.  Simmer 1 hour.  Strain and pour into cups.

name: Iced Tea Italiano
directions: Fill a rocks glass with ice. Add the amaro and lemon juice; stir in the Prosecco. Garnish with the wheel.

name: Parisian Hot Chocolate
directions: Heat the milk in a medium saucepan.
Once the milk is warm, whisk in the chocolate, stirring until melted and steaming hot.
For a thicker hot chocolate, cook at a very low boil for about 3 minutes, whisking frequently. Be careful and keep an eye on the mixture, as it may boil up a bit during the first moments.
Taste, and add brown sugar if desired.
Serve warm in small, demitasse cups.
Enjoy!
Note: This hot chocolate improves if made ahead and allowed to sit for a few hours. Rewarm before serving. David also likes to add a few flecks of fleur de sel, the very good sea salt from Brittany.

name: Alhambra Royale
directions: Drop the orange rind into the hot chocolate.  Heat the cognac in a metal ladle over boiling water or a gas flame and set it alight.  Pour it, flaming, into the chocolate.  Stir it in and spoon the whipped cream onto the surface of the cocktail.  Sprinkle with cocoa powder and enjoy.

name: Café Royale
directions: Rinse out four coffee cups with boiling water to warm the cups.  Pour out.  Add a sugar cube to each cup then fill a little below the rim with hot coffee.  Float 1 1/2 tbsp cognac on each of the four cups and set aflame.  Let flame for a few seconds then extinguish by stirring with a spoon.

similar.name: Brulot Charentais - Angel's Flames - French Flambé Coffee

name: Coffee and Chocolate Flip
directions: Separate the egg and beat the white until frothy and in another small bowl beat the egg yolk with the sugar.

In a small pan combine the brandy, Kahlúa, coffee and cream.  Warm over a very low heat then allow the mixture to cool.  When cool whisk this mixture into the egg yolk.  Add the egg white and pour the mixture back and forth between two glasses until it is smooth.
Pour into a tall glass over coarsely crushed ice and sprinkle the top with cocoa powder.

similar.name: Seize the Day Cafe Au Lait

name: Jerry’s Country Coffee for Hard Times
directions: Wash rye kernels.
Dry kernels in oven.
Roast kernels until dark in hot oven or brown dark. (Alternate quicker roasting method: in heavy pan on top of stove.).
Grind in coffee grinder and place grounds in filter in drip coffee maker basket.
Add water to coffee maker and start brewing!

similar.name: Polish Krupnik - Old Fashioned Honey and Spice Liqueur
The Original Shannon Airport Irish Coffee

name: Lavender, Chamomile and Jasmine Tea
directions: Blend together the loose black tea, lavender buds, chamomile flowers, and jasmine flowers.
Store in a tin container. Makes a nice gift!
Use 1 teaspoon of tea per 6-8 oz. of boiling water.

similar.name: Louisa: Greek Lemon Verbena Herbal Tea

name: Beautiful
directions: Layer the two ingredients in a short brandy snifter or cordial glass, and enjoy.

name: Tuscan Rosemary Lemon Drop
directions: Rosemary sugar: Mix the roasemary and sugare together in a small bowl, and spread the mixture on a rimmed baking sheet. Set in a warm dry place for about 4 days, until the rosemary is completely dried. Process in a food processsor or spice grinder until finely ground. Store in a tightly sealed container for up to one month at room temperature.
Rim a large martini glass with roesmary sugar, and set aside.

Bend 1 rosemary sprig and drop into cocktail shaker. Fill the shaker with ice. Measure in teh vodka, limoncello, lemon juice, and simple syrup. Cap and shake vigorously. Strain into the sugar-rimmed glass. Float a rosemary sprig in the drink for garnish.

name: French Pear Hot Toddy
directions: In a small saucepan, warm the Poire William and the brandy very gently over low heat.
Transfer to two large warmed balloon glasses.
Dissolve sugar in the hot coffee and pour into the liqueurs, stirring to combine.
Float the cream over the top of each toddy; garnish with a slice of fresh pear.
Do not stir – just sip and savor!

name: Wassail
directions: Combine sugar and water; boil together for 5 minutes.
Remove from heat.
Add spices, cover and allow to stand in warm place for 60 minutes.
Just before serving, add juices; bring to a rapid boil.
Remove from heat and serve.

similar.name: Polish Krupnik - Old Fashioned Honey and Spice Liqueur

name: In-A-Pinch Garlic Bread
directions: Preheat broiler.  Line cookie sheet with foil and spray with non-stick cooking spray.  Lay split rolls on cookie sheet for 6 pieces of bread.
Melt margarine in microwave for 1 minute on HIGH; remove from microwave and stir in garlic powder, cayenne, and Italian seasoning.
With pastry brush, brush on seasoned margarine evenly, coating each piece well.  Sprinkle with paprika.
Broil until well browned and bubbling, approximately 5 minutes, or to you liking.  Serve.

name: Caramel-Cream Macchiato Coffee
directions: Beat whipping cream, caramel topping and vanilla extract with an electric mixer on low speed until soft peaks form.
Divide the caramel-whipped cream into 2 mugs then pour hot brewed coffee over the top.

name: Bills Irish Coffee
directions: Brew Coffee. Run the orange around the top of the coffee cup and right away run the tops of the cups thru the 1 tablespoon Sugar. Then using a candle lighter or long lighter to heat the sugar on both sides till melted.pour coffee in then whiskey then cream if you are using it.Add wipped cream  and top cream with orange zest then enjoy.

name: Diy Galliano Liquer (Copycat)
directions: Make a sugar syrup by combining the sugar and water in a heavy saucepan and bringing to a boil for about a minute, then immediately reduce the heat.
Simmer for 1 hour or until thickened. Remove from heat and cool.
Pour the sugar syrup into a sterilized quart size bottle. Add the anise extract, vanilla, and food coloring.
Stir gently and add the vodka.
Cover and let the mixture sit for 10-14 days before serving.

name: Dancing Leprechaun
directions: Shake with ice.
Strain into glass with ice cubes.
Add a twist of lemon.

name: Bonnie Prince Charles
directions: Put all ingredients in a blender.
Blend until smooth.

name: Buck's Sunrise Fizz
directions: Frost 2 6 oz Champagne flute.
Carefully pour Grenadine into bottom of flute.
Add the orange juice.
Top off with the Champagne.

name: Greek Twister Shooter
directions: Layer in a shot glass.

name: Greek Matter Scatter
directions: Shake with ice and pour into a rocks glass or old-fashioned glass.

name: Ronague Bay Cocktail (Isle of Man)
directions: Fill a long glass with the ice.
Put whiskey, lime juice and sugar in a shaker.
Shake well.
Pour over ice.
Garnish with Lime slices.

name: Greek Goddess
directions: 1.  Pour the Curacao in a shot glass, then add the Ouzo. Enjoy!

name: Banana Cherry Cordial (Cocktail)
directions: In a cocktail shaker, add the chilled Jägermeister, banana liqueur, coconut rum and ouzo.  Shake a few times, just enough to mix ingredients.
Add ice to the shaker, then strain cocktail into a cocktail glass.
Carefully add the blue Curacao to the cocktail glass. Garnish with two cherries, and enjoy.

name: Slemp
directions: Tie saffron, cloves, cinnamon and mace in a piece of cheesecloth.
Place spice bundle and milk in a saucepan.
Heat to just under the boiling point and simmer for 30 minutes.
Add sugar.
Dissolve the cornstarch in a little water and add to milk mixture.
Stir and cook for 5 minutes more.
Press the spice bundle between two spoons, remove and serve.

name: Strawberry Orange Ade
directions: Chop strawberries and add to an 8-cup blender pitcher.
Add orange juice and lemon zest.
Strip basil leaves and blend with strawberries.
Garnish with a fresh basil leaf before serving over ice (or use frozen strawberries in step one for a nice chilly treat!).

name: Kitrón (Greek Lemon Liquor from Naxos)
directions: Wash lemons and lime under ho water.
Thinly peel them, avoiding the white part of the peel because it would make the liquor bitter.
Place the peel in a glass bottle or jar, fill in the Ouzo, seal the bottle and let stand in a cool lace for 12 days, shaking the bottle every now and then.
On day 13, heat water and sugar, bring to a rolling boil and boil for 3 minutes, then let cool completely.
Slowly pour ouzo from the bottle through a sieve lined with cheese cloth into a clean jar, then mix with sugar syrup and fill into a clean bottle.
The liquor will keep forever in the unlikely case that you don't drink it -- .

similar.name: Polish Krupnik - Old Fashioned Honey and Spice Liqueur

name: Gettin' Groggy With It (Ggwi)
directions: 1. Steep 4 tea bags in 1 quart hot water for 3 minutes.
2. Combine tea with cognac, rum, honey, freshly squeezed lemon juice, and cloves in a medium saucepan. Stir well.
3. Bring to a simmer over medium heat. Reduce heat to low and keep warm.
4. Serve in heatproof mugs and garnish with a pinch of ground nutmeg and a cinnamon stick.
